genre_refs: mid-century American storybook / Saturday Evening Post-style Christmas illustration; nostalgic Americana
description: A designed Christmas slogan graphic from the Department of Labor built over a nostalgic, mid-20th-century-style painted illustration of a snowy small-town winter scene. The artwork depicts a warmly lit brick rowhouse at night with snow falling; a family of carolers (man in a hat carrying a suitcase, a woman, and several children, one bundled holding a basket) walks up a snow-covered path toward an open doorway where a woman in a red dress welcomes them under a glowing porch lantern. A snowman, bare winter trees, and a decorated interior (visible Christmas tree through a window) complete the cozy, sentimental Americana mood; the palette is deep night-blues with golden warm window light. A small circular DOL/eagle seal sits at center above the text. Overlaid in large white serif script across the middle is the slogan 'Recapture the Dream.' The tweet text frames this as 'We won't stop fighting until we Recapture the American Dream for the AMERICAN People' with a Merry Christmas message. The technique fuses warm holiday nostalgia with the administration's 'American Dream' economic-nationalist branding, evoking an idealized white-picket, working-family past to be 'recaptured'; target audience is the agency's patriotic populist base.
